Main_Alt_3: There's no CEO to arrest. No headquarters to raid. No face to put on a wanted poster. The network runs itself, owned by no one, controlled by no one.
Main_Alt_4: This is actually one of the most important design decisions in Bitcoin's history — and it wasn't even technical. It was human.
R1_Alt_1: Satoshi understood that for a truly decentralised system to survive, there couldn't be a person at the centre of it.
R1_Alt_2: As soon as there's a leader, there's a target. Gold can be confiscated. Companies can be shut down.
R1_Alt_3: But you can't shut down a mathematical rulebook running across thousands of computers on every continent simultaneously.
R1_Alt_4: His disappearance wasn't a mystery to be solved — it may have been the whole point.
